look_at.text: A large leafy plant in the corner. It's doing better than most things in this building.

pick_up.text: I'm not stealing a plant. Even if no one would notice.

look_at.text: Another decorative plant by the door. Probably the most carefree thing in this reception area.

pick_up.text: I have enough to carry today without adding a plant.

look_at.text: An emergency procedure poster. Step 4 catches my eye: 'Staff must escort visitors inside in fire event.' Interesting policy.

use.text: The poster is fixed to the wall. I can read it though.

pick_up.text: Health and safety literature seems like something I should leave in place.

look_at.text: A security monitor showing two feeds: the lobby entrance, and the IT department — extension 204. Looks quiet in there.

use.text: I'm not a security guard. I just need to get inside.

look_at.text: A visitor badge printer blinking a red error light. The display reads: OUT OF PAPER. Typical IT response time: three days and counting.

use.text: The printer just blinks its red light at me. OUT OF PAPER. Not helpful.

pick_up.text: Stealing office equipment seems like a bad start to infiltrating this building.

look_at.text: A laminated sign propped on the desk. It reads: 'LOST YOUR BADGE? Please complete form IT-394b, have it countersigned by your line manager, your line manager's line manager, and one witness who has known you personally for at least three years. Processing time: 10–14 business days. In the meantime, please remain outside. Thank you for your understanding. Amazon cares about your security. ♥'

use.text: The sign has already said everything it has to say.

look_at.text: The receptionist's computer. The screen shows a visitor log system. My name isn't in it — obviously.

use.text: I can't just use someone else's workstation. Especially not right in front of them.

look_at.text: A wall-mounted fire alarm pull station. A label reads: 'Pull to Activate'. Another label reads: 'Do not tamper with safety equipment'.

use.text: The alarm is already blaring. I'm already inside.

effects.text: You grab the handle and pull hard. RIIIIING! The alarm blares throughout the building, emergency lights flashing red. The receptionist leaps to her feet.
Oh for— EVERYONE OUT! I— ...ugh. Fire protocol: I have to escort all visitors inside immediately.
She fixes you with a withering look.
Congratulations. You're officially a fire hazard. Come in.
You reach toward the alarm...
Hey! Hands off that! Tampering with safety equipment is grounds for immediate termination.

pull.text: The alarm is already going. No need.

effects.text: You grab the handle and pull hard. RIIIIING! The alarm blares throughout the building, emergency lights flashing red. The receptionist leaps to her feet.
Oh for— EVERYONE OUT! I— ...ugh. Fire protocol: I have to escort all visitors inside immediately.
She fixes you with a withering look.
Congratulations. You're officially a fire hazard. Come in.
You reach toward the alarm...
Hey! Hands off that! Tampering with safety equipment is grounds for immediate termination.

look_at.text: A 'Barista Pro' coffee machine with a display panel. Several drink options are available.

talk_to.text: The machine hums indifferently. I should USE it to order a drink.

pick_up.text: The machine is bolted to the counter.

look_at.text: A heavy door marked 'AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY'. It has a card reader on the side. This is exactly where I need to get through.

open.text: The door swings open. I'm in.
Locked. The card reader blinks red. I need to find another way through.

use.text: The door opens. I walk through.
The card reader blinks red. Access denied.

push.text: It doesn't budge. The lock holds firm.

talk_to.text: The door is unmoved by my conversation. Typical.

look_at.text: She's sipping the cappuccino contentedly, eyes slightly glazed. Distracted. Good.
The receptionist sits behind the desk with the focused energy of someone running on zero sleep and sheer professionalism.

effects.text: She waves a hand.
Mm. Busy sipping. Give me a moment.

give.text: I should use an item from my inventory on her.

combinations.text: You slide the cappuccino across the desk. Her eyes lock onto it instantly.

effects.text: "Ah... finally. The perfect caffeine balance."
"Okay. I can handle a small distraction now."

combinations.text: You place the cappuccino on the desk in front of her. She stares at it.

effects.text: "Ah... finally. The perfect caffeine balance."
"Okay. I can handle a small distraction now."

combinations.text: You slide the business card across the desk with quiet confidence.

effects.text: "...Jeff Bezos. CEO Amazon." She looks up slowly.
"I'm going to need a little more than a business card to believe you're Jeff Bezos."

combinations.text: You slide the business card across the desk with quiet confidence.

effects.text: "...Jeff Bezos. CEO Amazon." She looks up slowly.
"I'm going to need a little more than a business card to believe you're Jeff Bezos."

combinations.text: You offer her the stress ball. She eyes it for a moment.

effects.text: "A stress ball? How thoughtful."
"Working at Amazon is never stressful. This will go straight to lost property."

combinations.text: You place the stress ball on the desk. She stares at it.

effects.text: "A stress ball? How thoughtful."
"Working at Amazon is never stressful. This will go straight to lost property."
